method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA). The public site www.nychealthandhospitals.org is behind a Radware Bot Manager (server: rdwr; 302 to validate.perfdrive.com), so HTML crawling is blocked at the edge. Fingerprinted the MyChart patient portal (mychart.nychhc.org → Microsoft-IIS/10.0, MyChartPersistence cookie = Epic MyChart) and, most importantly, discovered the LIVE Epic FHIR R4 / SMART on FHIR API for H+H via Epic's public endpoint directory (open.epic.com/Endpoints/R4): base https://epicproxypda.nychhc.org/FHIRProxy/api/FHIR/R4. Pulled its unauthenticated CapabilityStatement (fhirVersion 4.0.1, software 'Epic November 2025', 59 resource types) and .well-known/smart-configuration (OAuth2 authorize+token, SMART capabilities). Verified via the Socrata Discovery API that NO NYC Open Data dataset is owned by H+H (agency labels 'NYC Health + Hospitals' and 'Health and Hospitals Corporation (HHC)' both return 0; health datasets on data.cityofnewyork.us belong to DOHMH).
headline_findings: H+H is the FIRST domain in this project with a real, live, standards-based API already in production: an Epic FHIR R4 / SMART on FHIR endpoint (epicproxypda.nychhc.org, 'Epic November 2025') exposing 59 resource types with an OAuth2 authorize/token surface. The health-data standard exists — the work is not to invent an API.

But that FHIR surface is read-mostly and patient-scoped. Appointment is read + search only; there is NO Slot resource and NO $book/$find operation, so BOOKING has no machine-readable contract even inside the standard. Location/Organization/Practitioner exist but require SMART authorization (unauthenticated Location search returns HTTP 401).

There is no OPEN directory. A patient or agent asking 'which H+H hospital near me has cardiology and takes new patients?' has nothing to call — the facility/service/provider directory is HTML on a bot-walled marketing site, not on FHIR (auth-gated) and not on Open Data.

H+H publishes NOTHING to NYC Open Data. Its data is clinical and lives in Epic (HIPAA-protected); the health datasets on data.cityofnewyork.us belong to a different agency, DOHMH. See opendata-hhc.md.

Everything runs on a vendor EHR. The FHIR API, the MyChart portal, and the OAuth surface are all Epic — H+H owns the data but not the contract. The site is protected by Radware Bot Manager, a third vendor.

Net-new write surface: AppointmentRequest — request/book an appointment — the most common patient transaction, with no API today and outside the live FHIR surface. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: THE real H+H API. CapabilityStatement: fhirVersion 4.0.1, software 'Epic November 2025', 59 resource types (Patient, Condition, Observation, Appointment, Location, Organization, Practitioner, MedicationRequest, DocumentReference, ...). OAuth2 authorize/token at /FHIRProxy/oauth2. Appointment is read+search only; no Slot, no $book, no open directory (Location search → 401).

Epic MyChart on Microsoft-IIS/10.0 (MyChartPersistence cookie). The human UI over the same Epic system — where patients see appointments, message providers, refill prescriptions, pay bills. No open API of its own; the machine surface is the FHIR endpoint above.
Epic's public FHIR endpoint directory (480 orgs); lists 'NYC Health + Hospitals' → the FHIRProxy base URL. How the H+H FHIR endpoint is discoverable at all.
Marketing/directory site (locations, services, doctors, help-paying-your-bill). Behind Radware Bot Manager (server: rdwr, 302 to validate.perfdrive.com) — crawl blocked at the edge. No content API.
Health datasets here belong to the Department of Health and Mental Hygiene (DOHMH). H+H owns ZERO datasets. Adjacent, not owned.

fruit.name: Find a location / facility
fruit.count: 11 acute-care hospitals + Gotham Health FQHCs + post-acute/nursing + behavioral + Correctional Health
fruit.notes: The core physical unit. FHIR Location/Organization exist but require auth (401 unauthenticated); no open directory, no Open Data twin. FHIR-aligned in facility.json.

fruit.name: Service lines & specialties
fruit.notes: Clinical service lines (primary care, cardiology, behavioral health, maternity, gender-affirming care...). HTML only on the bot-walled site; FHIR-aligned to HealthcareService (not exposed). No Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Find a doctor / provider
fruit.notes: Find a Doctor. FHIR Practitioner + PractitionerRole exist on the endpoint (read+search) but auth-gated; no open provider directory, no Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Financial assistance / NYC Care / sliding-scale
fruit.notes: H+H is the safety-net system: NYC Care (1-646-NYC-CARE), the HHC Options sliding scale, and financial counseling — eligibility immigration-status-blind. HTML only; no API; no FHIR analogue; no Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Pharmacy
fruit.notes: Outpatient / retail pharmacies at facilities; refills run through MyChart. FHIR-aligned to Location; no open directory, no Open Data twin.

fruit.name: My appointments (MyChart)
fruit.notes: A patient's booked appointments — visible in MyChart and, uniquely in this project, ALSO readable via the live FHIR Appointment resource under SMART patient authorization. Read-only: no create, no Slot, no $book on the H+H endpoint.

fruit.name: Request / book an appointment
fruit.notes: The single most common patient transaction — request/book an appointment (call 1-844-NYC-4NYC or a MyChart flow). NO public API and, notably, NO contract even in the live FHIR surface (no Appointment create, no Slot, no $book). The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

meta.platform: Epic (MyChart on IIS + live FHIR R4 / SMART on FHIR) behind Radware Bot Manager
meta.netnew: AppointmentRequest — request/book an appointment (no write in the live FHIR surface)
meta.tagline: A real health-data standard (Epic FHIR R4 / SMART on FHIR) is already live here — the work is to extend it to booking and open the facility directory, not to invent an API.
meta.headline: H+H is the first NYC domain with a live, standards-based API, but it is read-mostly, patient-scoped, vendor-owned Epic FHIR — with no open directory and no way to book an appointment.

scorecard_notes.platform: Best platform in the series: production Epic FHIR R4 / SMART on FHIR + MyChart — a real, modern, standards-based stack.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: Public site is bot-walled (Radware); no content/directory API of any kind.
scorecard_notes.openData: Zero datasets owned by H+H on NYC Open Data; health data there belongs to DOHMH.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Rich, standardized FHIR core model (59 resources) — but patient-scoped, no bulk/open access, and no open facility/service/provider directory.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: FHIR exposes Patient.create only; the meaningful transaction — booking — has no create, no Slot, no $book.
scorecard_notes.agent: Machine-readable FHIR standard exists but nothing agent-native (no MCP); directory is HTML behind a bot wall.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Standards-based but entirely vendor-run — the API, portal, and OAuth are all Epic; H+H owns the data, not the contract.
